task_completion.description: Open the draft document in OpenOffice Writer and enable Track Changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenOffice Writer is open displaying the proposal document. Evidence of 'Track Changes' being enabled can be seen either through the Edit > Changes menu or by the presence of tracked edits (colored text, underlines, strikethroughs) in the document.

task_completion.description: Correct the typo 'Drosphila' to 'Drosophila' and ensure it is italicized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'Drosophila' is spelled correctly and italicized. Because track changes is on, the new word should appear as an insertion (e.g., colored and underlined) and the old typo 'Drosphila' as a deletion (strikethrough).

task_completion.description: Delete the sentence about winning a Nobel prize.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sentence 'This experiment will be totally awesome and win a Nobel prize.' is marked as deleted, typically shown with a strikethrough and in a different color.

task_completion.description: Add a comment to the phrase '95% efficiency rate' requesting a citation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A comment box is visible in the margin connected to the phrase '95% efficiency rate'. The comment text must state 'Please provide a citation for this figure.'

task_completion.description: Add a comment to the document title stating it is too vague.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A comment box is visible in the margin connected to the document title ('Proposal: Genetics Study'). The comment text must state 'Title is too vague. Be more specific.'
The OpenOffice Writer window title bar shows 'proposal_reviewed.odt' or there is a 'Save As' dialog saving the file with this exact name in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenOffice Writer GUI to make edits and comments.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenOffice Writer interface, rather than using terminal commands, scripts, or a raw text/XML editor to modify the .odt file.

integrity.description: Edits were made using the built-in Track Changes feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: The edits appear as genuine tracked changes (standard OpenOffice formatting for insertions/deletions) rather than manually applied font colors or strikethrough formatting.

integrity.description: Comments were added using the built-in Comment feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: The comments are created using the Insert > Comment feature (appearing as standard margin comment boxes with author metadata), not just text typed directly into the document body or as floating text boxes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using Apache OpenOffice Writer's Track Changes feature (Edit > Changes > Record). The original document contains the typo 'Drosphila', the sentence 'This experiment will be totally awesome and win a Nobel prize.', and the phrase '95% efficiency rate'. The document title is 'Proposal: Genetics Study'. Tracked changes typically appear as colored text with underlines for insertions and strikethroughs for deletions. Comments appear as boxes in the right margin. The output file must be saved as 'proposal_reviewed.odt'.
